skill.name: brand-guidelines
skill.description: Applies Anthropic brand colors, typography, and visual styling guidance to artifacts that need a consistent Anthropic look and feel.
skill.summary: Applies Anthropic brand colors and typography guidance to visual artifacts.
skill.author: Azeem-2

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported high-risk patterns, but review found no executable code, network behavior, or malicious instructions. The flagged items are Apache license URLs, Markdown color literals, typography text, and ordinary prose. This is low risk and safe to publish from a security perspective.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Apache License URLs
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL findings point to standard Apache License reference links. They are license text, not runtime network calls or data exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both locations are inside the Apache License text. No code path exists that could open these URLs.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts are matches inside license prose and brand guidance text. No hashing, encryption, digest generation, or cryptographic API usage appears in the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows natural-language license and design text at every location. There are no cryptographic operations to exploit.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Around Colors
low_findings.description: The external command alerts are Markdown inline code markers around color hex values. They do not request Ruby execution, shell execution, or command invocation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines only list brand color values in documentation. No interpreter or command runner is referenced.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Terms in Design Text
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts match design terminology and library naming. Line 23 describes a gray color, and line 72 mentions RGBColor for precise color matching.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is visual styling guidance. No system probing, host enumeration, or network scanning instruction is present.

content.value_statement: Teams often need brand-consistent visuals without manually checking every color and font choice. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code to apply Anthropic-style colors, typography, and accents.
actual_capabilities: Lists the Anthropic-inspired main palette with dark, light, gray, and accent colors.
Specifies Poppins for headings and Lora for body text, with common fallback fonts.
Guides heading and body text styling based on size and hierarchy.
Recommends accent color cycling for non-text shapes and supporting elements.
Describes how to preserve readability while applying brand colors.
limitations: It does not include executable styling scripts or automated file conversion tools.
It depends on available fonts when a target environment lacks Poppins or Lora.
It does not verify official brand approval for public or commercial artifacts.
It focuses on visual styling, not messaging, legal review, or trademark clearance.

use_cases.title: Brand a Presentation
use_cases.description: Apply consistent colors, fonts, and accents to slides that need an Anthropic visual style.

use_cases.title: Style a Design Mockup
use_cases.description: Give interface concepts a clear palette and typography direction before visual review.

use_cases.title: Format Internal Artifacts
use_cases.description: Make reports, diagrams, and handouts feel visually consistent across a team.

prompt_templates.title: Apply Basic Brand Styling
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a simple artifact refresh.
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the brand-guidelines skill to this artifact. Update colors, typography, and accents while preserving the original structure.

prompt_templates.title: Refresh a Slide Deck
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this slide deck and apply Anthropic-style brand colors, heading fonts, body fonts, and accent colors. Keep the content unchanged.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Visual Consistency
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for review before publishing.
prompt_templates.prompt: Check this artifact against the brand-guidelines skill. Identify mismatched colors, typography issues, weak contrast, and inconsistent accent use.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Brand Application Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a structured redesign plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a step-by-step plan to restyle this multi-page artifact with the brand-guidelines skill. Include palette usage, typography hierarchy, and shape accents.

output_examples.input: A presentation uses mixed blues, default fonts, and inconsistent heading sizes.
output_examples.output: The revised direction uses dark text on light backgrounds, Poppins-style headings, Lora-style body text, and measured orange, blue, and green accents.

output_examples.input: A product brief needs a more polished brand treatment.
output_examples.output: The brief receives a consistent text hierarchy, restrained gray backgrounds, readable contrast, and accent colors for callouts and supporting shapes.

output_examples.input: A diagram has many unrelated colors and hard-to-read labels.
output_examples.output: The diagram is simplified with the main palette, clear label contrast, and repeated accent colors that support scanning.

best_practices: Use the palette to support hierarchy, not to decorate every element.
Check contrast after applying light backgrounds, dark text, and accent colors.
Keep content structure stable when the task is only visual restyling.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the guidance as legal permission to use Anthropic trademarks.
Do not force custom fonts when the target environment cannot render them.
Do not replace clear information design with excessive accent color usage.

faq.question: Does this skill install fonts?
faq.answer: No. It recommends Poppins and Lora, with Arial and Georgia as fallbacks.

faq.question: Can it edit presentations automatically?
faq.answer: The skill provides styling guidance. Any automated edits depend on the host tool and available file handlers.

faq.question: Is this official Anthropic brand approval?
faq.answer: No. It is community-provided guidance and does not replace official approval or legal review.

faq.question: Which artifacts fit this skill?
faq.answer: It fits slides, reports, diagrams, mockups, and other visual artifacts that need consistent styling.

faq.question: Does it make network requests?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The reviewed files contain documentation and license text only.

faq.question: Can it work in Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
